engineering.translation: The one whose happiness is within (antar-sukhaḥ), whose joy is within (antar-ārāmaḥ), whose light is within (antar-jyotiḥ). That yogi, having become Brahman (brahma-bhūtaḥ), attains brahma-nirvāṇa (terminal liberation in/as Brahman). The verse names the inner-located triple — happiness, joy, light — and ties this internal location to brahma-nirvāṇa. STRETCHED honestly. Brahma-nirvāṇa exceeds operational reach; the engineering analog scopes downward. The senior whose satisfaction, joy, and clarity sit inside (per 5.21) — not in external contact (per 5.22) — has reached the operating rung. The metaphysical destination is past where engg travels.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A principal engineer at twenty-five years tenure. Her teammates on the Datadog-Slack stack observe: across the last five years, the source of her satisfaction has been internal (the work itself), the source of her joy has been internal (engagement with Kubernetes-scale architecture problems on GitHub). The source of her clarity has been internal (the substrate-knowledge cultivated across the Postgres-Datadog-Kafka stack). External Slack praise does not anchor competence; launch-tweet receptions do not source her joy; executive recognition does not source her clarity. The triple — antar-sukha, antar-ārāma, antar-jyoti — is operationally readable in her demeanour. STRETCHED honestly because brahma-nirvāṇa names a metaphysical maximum the engineering analog cannot fully reach. The operational rung is her settled inner state across decades; the transcendent destination is past where this layer can describe. Tools she trains on daily: Postgres, Kafka, Redis, Kubernetes, Datadog, Sentry, GitHub, the deploy pipeline, the merge queue, the CI runner, the CD pipeline, the PR review tooling.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader concludes that engineering tenure produces brahma-nirvāṇa. It does not. The verse's reach is metaphysically far greater than the engineering analog can claim. An engineer who reads the verse as 'become a senior and you become liberated' has misread it.
engineering.counter_example: When a senior's apparent inner-source is in fact rigidity — refusal to update under new evidence, refusal to acknowledge evolving understanding, closed to junior corrections — the verse does not endorse this. brahma-nirvāṇa, even at the operational rung, is settled-and-receiving, not settled-and-closed.
engineering.implication: Audit, across a quarter, whether your satisfaction-joy-clarity sources are internal or external. The internal triple is the operational rung; brahma-nirvāṇa is past where engineering travels.
engineering.quotable_line: Inner happiness, inner joy, inner light. The operational rung; brahma-nirvāṇa is past where engineering travels.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 5.24 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored to chapter-5 thesis: equivalence-of-paths-with-karma-yoga-preference, decoupled identity, sama-darśana, internal-bliss-source, brahma-nirvāṇa-as-operational-rung-with-honest-STRETCHED. Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.
